response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack engineer, but their background is almost entirely web, mobile, and backend work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which does not align with this embedded firmware security role. The position has hard requirements the candidate clearly does not meet: native-level Japanese (the resume lists only English, Romanian, and basic German), strong C proficiency for UNIX development (only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a C++ thesis), and Linux kernel investigation with source modification (no evidence). There is also no exposure to LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 networking, or chip-vendor SDKs. The mismatch is fundamental rather than a matter of seniority.
strengths: 10+ years of professional software development experience, well above the 3-year minimum
Some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, C++ thesis) and comfort with Linux
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, and prior hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, device orchestration)
concerns: No Japanese language ability, which is a stated native-level hard requirement
Minimal C experience and no Linux kernel modification or embedded firmware background
No security or networking domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch SDKs)
